title: Hospital Discharge Summary — Post-Accident
subtitle: Synthetic discharge summary for a road accident patient at District Hospital Dharamgarh
rawText:
DISTRICT HOSPITAL DHARAMGARH
KALAHANDI, ODISHA — 766015

DISCHARGE SUMMARY

Patient Name: [REDACTED — Synthetic Data]
Age/Sex: 42/M
IP No.: DH/DHR/2026/IPD/4521
Date of Admission: 10.06.2026
Date of Discharge: 12.06.2026
Ward: Male Surgical (IPD)

DIAGNOSIS: Closed fracture of right tibia (mid-shaft) with contusions and abrasions over right lower limb. Road Traffic Accident (RTA) on NH-26 near Dharamgarh.

HISTORY: Patient was brought to the Emergency Department via 108 ambulance following a two-wheeler vs. truck collision on NH-26. Patient was conscious and oriented at the time of admission. Complained of severe pain in right leg with inability to bear weight. No loss of consciousness. No hemoptysis. No abdominal distension noted.

INVESTIGATIONS:
- X-ray right leg (AP and Lateral): Transverse fracture of right tibia at junction of middle and distal third. No involvement of fibula.
- CBC: Hemoglobin 11.2 g/dL (mildly low), TLC 9,800/cumm, Platelet count 2.1 lakh/cumm
- Blood Group: B+ve
- RFT: Blood Urea 28 mg/dL, Serum Creatinine 0.9 mg/dL — within normal limits

TREATMENT GIVEN:
- Below-knee POP cast applied to right lower limb
- Inj. Diclofenac 75mg IM BD for pain management (analgesic)
- Inj. Cefuroxime 1.5g IV BD (prophylaxis against infection)
- Tab. Pantoprazole 40mg OD (gastroprotection)
- Tetanus Toxoid booster administered

DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
1. Tab. Diclofenac Sodium 50mg — 1 tablet twice daily after food for 7 days (analgesic/antipyretic)
2. Tab. Cefuroxime 500mg — 1 tablet twice daily after food for 5 days (antibiotic)
3. Tab. Pantoprazole 40mg — 1 tablet once daily before breakfast for 7 days (acid reducer)
4. Tab. Calcium + Vitamin D3 — 1 tablet once daily for 30 days
5. Syp. Lactulose 15ml at bedtime if constipated (due to reduced mobility)

ADVICE ON DISCHARGE:
- Strict bed rest with right leg elevated on 2 pillows for minimum 2 weeks
- Do NOT bear weight on right leg until cleared by orthopedic surgeon
- Keep POP cast dry at all times. Do not insert objects inside the cast.
- Report immediately if you notice: increased swelling of toes, numbness/tingling in foot, foul smell from cast, fever above 100.4°F, or worsening pain despite medication
- Follow-up with Orthopedic OPD within 48 hours for cast check and further management plan
- Bring this discharge summary, X-ray films, and all investigation reports to follow-up visit
- Submit copy of this discharge summary to your insurance provider within 7 days for claim processing

CONDITION AT DISCHARGE: Stable. Patient counseled regarding cast care and danger signs.

Discharged against medical advice: No

Consultant: Dr. [REDACTED]
Designation: Senior Medical Officer, Orthopedics

(This is a synthetic document created for demonstration purposes. No real patient data is used.)
